name: Hookdeck Webhook Management API
what_it_does: Manages inbound webhooks from third-party services with reliable queuing, filtering, transformation, fan-out routing, and automatic retry on delivery failures.
use_cases: Receive webhooks from Stripe, GitHub, or other SaaS tools and route them to agent pipelines without missing events during downtime
Filter and transform webhook payloads before they reach an agent so the agent only processes relevant, normalized events
Fan-out a single inbound webhook event to multiple downstream agent workers or endpoints simultaneously
Replay failed or missed webhook deliveries to an agent endpoint after a bug fix or deployment
Inspect and debug full webhook request/response history to diagnose why an agent pipeline processed an event incorrectly
not_for: Sending outbound webhooks to your own customers' endpoints (use Svix instead)
Real-time sub-100ms pub/sub messaging between services (use Ably or Pusher)
General-purpose task queues or job scheduling (use a dedicated queue like SQS or BullMQ)
best_when: You need a reliable inbound webhook gateway that decouples third-party event producers from your agent consumers with filtering, transformation, and replay capabilities.
avoid_when: Your primary need is sending webhooks outbound to customers rather than receiving them from external services.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token. Inbound webhook sources use auto-generated signing secrets per source for verification. Team-level and project-level key scoping available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 100,000 events per month, 3-day event retention, 1 workspace
pricing.paid_tiers: Developer $25/mo, Team $100/mo, Business $500/mo; enterprise custom; scales on event volume and retention
pricing.notes: Free tier sufficient for development. Retention period and event volume are the main upgrade drivers for production workloads.

performance.rate_limits: Ingest: effectively unlimited at the source URL; delivery API: ~600 req/min default; replay and management APIs have lower limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields. Dashboard UI provides detailed delivery attempt logs. Webhook verification errors are clearly surfaced.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Hookdeck assigns a unique event ID to each inbound webhook; agents can use this for deduplication. No idempotency key on outbound management API calls.
known_agent_gotchas: Connections (source + destination pairs) must be pre-configured; agents cannot dynamically create routing on the fly without an API call to create the connection first
Transformation functions are JavaScript snippets executed server-side; agents calling the API to deploy transformations must validate JS syntax before submission
Event retention limits mean older events cannot be replayed on free/lower tiers — agents that need audit replay should check plan limits
Fan-out to multiple destinations requires separate connection objects per destination, not a single multi-target configuration
Rate limiting on the management API is separate from the ingest rate; agents that programmatically create many connections may hit management API limits

security_score_components.security_notes: Per-source signing secrets enable inbound signature verification. Secrets are not exposed after initial creation. TLS enforced on all endpoints.
